item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This definitely has potential and the quality is amazing but why would it classified as an SCP? Why does it attract people to the itself? It just seems like a regular squid tbh. Its capabilities are a bit vague so maybe add something else along with the vocalizations.

item.title: endearing characters
item.content:encoded:
Roughly an hour passes as the Nautilus descends towards the Bathyal zone, stopping periodically for depressurisation. Onboard crew can be heard chatting about personal matters. Communication resumes once the Nautilus reaches a depth of roughly one thousand meters.

I think writing in some of that personal chatter would help endear the characters to the reader and allow you to establish the relationship between the characters early on.
As they delve deeper into the ocean the reader could delve deeper into their romantic relationship and their struggles.
Maybe their relationship is in a bad place but they have to be professional and work together for the research project. That would add subtext to the silent moments when they weren't talking. In the end when they start talking about how they love each other it could be attributed to the cognitohazard and hint at 4687's process of reproduction.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
sometimes when I read an article all I can think of is it's meme potential
a squid that makes people and fish swim down
The finding nemo memes write themselves
The audio controls were very user friendly. I appreciate that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey there! Thanks for the feedback. I'm going to compile yours and any other comments I get and take them back to the drawing board when I come around for my second attempt.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not really feeling this one, sorry.

The first line of your special containment procedures feels like it belongs in the description, and to a degree, it's re-iterated. It's not actionable, just raw information. If you said "containment efforts will be centered around x, x, and y, the primary habitat of-" then it might work.

Second, it feels as though you reveal the anomaly too quickly, and too slowly all at once. You spend a great deal of time describing these squid creatures before telling us why they're unique, but then once you do, nothing much else is revealed. The potential twist that they may be intelligent, or at least pack-based predators, doesn't really add much to the story. There's no big twist.

Finally, the dialogue feels realistic, even military, but it's not very engaging. The old cliche of "oh god, oh god" and a quick emotional tie-in at the end doesn't endear the characters to me at all.

That said, it's all quite well written and formatted, and the core concept has teeth - but without proper execution, narrative, and twist, there's no actual bite. I'll have to downvote for now, but it shows promise.

item.title: Credit + Acknowledgements
item.content:encoded:
Hello! This one took kind of a while between story tweaks and crappy audio drafts.

Huge thanks to cybersqyd, caspian2, YellowCorp and The Great Hippo for feedback and general writing advice. This would have died on the ideas shelf if it weren't for these awesome writers, so big thanks to all of you!

Here are the image credits:
First: Public domain. https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/14495676#page/57/mode/1up
Second: Credit to aemalkin on Flickr. https://www.flickr.com/photos/aemalkin/2896868192/in/photostream/
